items.name: typography
items.title: Typography
items.description: Semantic heading, text, and inline code primitives with System UI as the Core default and optional consumer-loaded Geist, Inter, IBM Plex, Manrope, Source Sans 3, and Space Grotesk preset recipes.
accessibility: Use Heading as the correct semantic level for the document outline, not merely for visual size.
Use Text for supporting copy without replacing native labels or accessible names.
Use Code only for short inline code; use pre and code for larger code blocks.
Typography presets are scoped CSS token recipes; load Geist or Inter in the consuming product before applying their preset class.

items.name: kbd
items.title: Kbd
items.description: Displays a keyboard shortcut with native kbd semantics.
accessibility: Renders the native kbd element so shortcut notation retains semantic meaning.
Use Kbd as supplemental shortcut notation, not as the only way to discover a command.
When Kbd is displayed inside a named control, hide it from assistive technology to avoid repeating the action name.
items.usage:
import { Kbd } from '@/components/nerio/components/kbd';

<Kbd>⌘S</Kbd>

items.name: button
items.title: Button
items.description: Triggers an explicit action with text, optional Badge content, directional icons, shortcut hints, tooltip help, or an icon-only mode.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Button primitive and renders a native button by default.
For navigation, render Button as a native anchor with variant link; it has no control padding or underline.
Provide concise visible text or an accessible name.
Loading preserves the action name, disables repeat activation, and exposes aria-busy; loadingLabel is deprecated.
Disabled and loading states prevent repeat activation.
Icons render through the Nerio icon adapter and remain decorative by default.
For an icon-only Button, provide icon and an aria-label; tooltip can add supplementary help but does not replace the action name.
Shortcut display is supplemental; add aria-keyshortcuts yourself when a keyboard shortcut is functional.
Badge content remains part of the Button accessible name; use a concise count or status that refines the action.
Deprecated subtle and destructive variants normalize to secondary and danger; use the canonical variants in new code.
Keep the visible focus treatment enabled.

items.name: icon-button
items.title: IconButton
items.description: Deprecated compatibility wrapper for Button icon-only mode.
accessibility: Requires a label and forwards it as the accessible name for Button icon-only mode.
Deprecated compatibility wrapper: use Button with icon and aria-label in new code.
This registry item remains installable until the next major release.

items.name: button-group
items.title: ButtonGroup
items.description: Joins related Buttons into one attached horizontal control set.
accessibility: Renders a group landmark by default; provide aria-label for its shared purpose.
Preserves the native semantics and keyboard behavior of every child Button.
Use the same Button variant for every child so the actions remain visually equal.

items.name: badge
items.title: Badge
items.description: Labels status, category, or lightweight metadata.
accessibility: Use concise text that does not rely on color alone.
Avoid using Badge as a button or link.
Use tone for semantic color; variant remains a deprecated compatibility alias.
Optional icons are decorative, so text must carry the status meaning.
Use leadingIcon or trailingIcon for visual context; the label remains the accessible status name.
Use sm when the Badge is embedded inside a compact control such as Button.
Use strong emphasis only when a warning, danger, or other status needs elevated attention.

items.name: spinner
items.title: Spinner
items.description: Indicates a short loading moment.
accessibility: Expose a label when loading status is not otherwise announced.
Use for short waits and prefer Skeleton when layout should be reserved.
Do not use several independent spinners in the same region.
items.usage:
import { Spinner } from '@/components/nerio/components/spinner';

<Spinner label="Loading activity" />

items.name: skeleton
items.title: Skeleton
items.description: Reserves layout while content is loading.
accessibility: Skeleton is hidden from assistive technology by default.
Pair loading regions with an accessible status when the wait is meaningful.
Reserve stable dimensions to avoid layout shift.
items.usage:
import { Skeleton } from '@/components/nerio/components/skeleton';

<Skeleton />

items.name: empty-state
items.title: EmptyState
items.description: Explains missing content and offers a next action when useful.
accessibility: Use a clear title and description instead of decorative empty copy.
Provide a primary action and an optional secondary action only when there is an obvious recovery path.
Do not use toasts to explain an empty page state.
items.usage:
import { EmptyState } from '@/components/nerio/components/empty-state';

<EmptyState title="No collections" description="Create one to start organizing work." action={<Button>Create collection</Button>} secondaryAction={<Button variant="ghost">Learn more</Button>} />

items.name: alert
items.title: Alert
items.description: Communicates inline feedback with a clear tone.
accessibility: Static inline alerts do not announce by default; pass role="status" or role="alert" only for dynamic updates that need announcement.
Tone is supported by text; do not rely on color alone.
Optional icons must pass through the Nerio icon adapter.

items.name: card
items.title: Card
items.description: Groups related product information on a quiet surface.
accessibility: Use Card to group related content, not to decorate every section.
Provide semantic headings inside cards when the content needs structure.
Use CardVisual for icons, avatars, logos, previews, and media; only bleed placement reaches the card edge.
Use CardAction only in a surface Card. Linked Cards cannot contain interactive descendants.
Avoid nesting cards inside cards.
Use href for a whole-card destination; it renders a native anchor with hover and focus feedback. Do not place interactive descendants inside a linked Card.
items.usage:
import { Card, CardAction, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardVisual } from '@/components/nerio/components/card'; ...
Project summary Current status, owner, and next action.
... Ready for review

items.name: separator
items.title: Separator
items.description: Divides related sections without adding visual noise.
accessibility: Renders an hr element for semantic separation.
Use for related sections, not as a replacement for spacing.
Keep separators subtle across themes.
items.usage:
import { Separator } from '@/components/nerio/components/separator';

<Separator />

items.name: avatar
items.title: Avatar
items.description: Identifies a person, team, or entity.
accessibility: Use the name prop to generate fallback initials.
Non-decorative images and fallbacks receive the supplied alt text or normalized name; decorative fallbacks are hidden.
Do not rely on Avatar alone for critical identification in dense lists.
items.usage:
import { Avatar } from '@/components/nerio/components/avatar';

<Avatar name="Maya Chen" />

items.name: progress
items.title: Progress
items.description: Shows completion for a task, upload, or workflow.
accessibility: Clamps aria-valuenow between 0 and 100 and exposes an accessible progressbar name.
Provide a label when the surrounding context does not name the progress.
Omit value for indeterminate progress when a reliable value is not available.
items.usage:
import { Progress } from '@/components/nerio/components/progress';

<Progress label="Completion" value={68} />

items.name: stat
items.title: Stat
items.description: Highlights a compact product metric.
accessibility: Use clear labels so values remain understandable out of context.
Keep trend text textual; do not encode direction by color alone.
Use advanced KPI cards outside the core primitive layer.
items.usage:
import { Stat } from '@/components/nerio/components/stat';

<Stat label="Active projects" value="12" trend="+3 this week" />

items.name: key-value
items.title: KeyValue
items.description: Displays compact metadata as a label and value pair.
accessibility: Renders a native dl with dt and dd children for one metadata pair.
Keep labels concise and stable.
Do not use for long prose values.
items.usage:
import { KeyValue } from '@/components/nerio/components/key-value';

<KeyValue label="Owner" value="Product team" />

items.name: table
items.title: Table
items.description: Presents structured records for scanning and comparison.
accessibility: Use semantic table sections, headers, rows, and cells.
Keep sorting, filtering, pagination, selection, and advanced table behavior out of this Core primitive.
TableContainer is plain by default; labelled regions are optional, while focusable scroll regions require a concise label.
Keep dense operational tables scannable with clear column labels.
items.usage: import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableContainer, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from '@/components/nerio/components/table'; NameRoadmap

items.name: list
items.title: List
items.description: Presents simple structured lists with optional descriptions, metadata, and native links.
accessibility: Uses semantic ul by default and semantic ol when ordered is true.
Items with href render native anchors instead of fake interactive containers.
Optional description and metadata remain in the natural reading order.
linkProps can extend class, events, target, rel, aria, and data attributes; item.href and the internal data-slot remain protected.
Do not use List for selectable listbox, command menu, or data table behavior.
items.usage:
import { List } from '@/components/nerio/components/list';

<List items={[{ title: 'Tokens', description: 'CSS variable foundation for themes, modes, and density.', href: '/docs/foundations/tokens' }, { title: 'Components', description: 'Composable Core primitives installed as source.', href: '/docs/components/button' }]} />

items.name: select
items.title: Select
items.description: Chooses one option from a compact list.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Select primitive.
The visible label is associated with the select control.
Supports placeholder, description, helper or error message, disabled, and invalid states.
Supports native form metadata including name, form, required, readOnly, and autoComplete.
Does not select the first option when only a placeholder is supplied.
Error messages are announced with role="alert" only when invalid is true.
Options support disabled states and keyboard navigation.
items.usage:
import { Select } from '@/components/nerio/components/select';

<Select label="Status" name="status" placeholder="Choose status" message="Choose the closest workflow state." options={[{ label: 'Active', value: 'active' }]} />

items.name: radio-group
items.title: RadioGroup
items.description: Chooses one option from a small visible set.
accessibility: Uses Base UI Radio Group and Radio primitives for keyboard behavior.
Associates the visible group label with aria-labelledby.
Connects description and message text through aria-describedby.
Supports disabled options, required, invalid, controlled, and uncontrolled values.
Validation messages use role="alert" only when invalid is true.
items.usage:
import { RadioGroup } from '@/components/nerio/components/radio-group';

<RadioGroup label="Visibility" name="visibility" defaultValue="team" options={[{ label: 'Private', value: 'private' }, { label: 'Team', value: 'team' }]} />

items.name: input
items.title: Input
items.description: Collects short text values in forms and filters.
accessibility: Use with Label or Field so the control has an accessible name.
Supports native input attributes including required, disabled, autocomplete, and aria-describedby.
Invalid state maps to aria-invalid.
Disabled state uses the native disabled attribute.

items.name: textarea
items.title: Textarea
items.description: Collects longer notes, descriptions, and comments.
accessibility: Use with Label or Field so the control has an accessible name.
Supports native textarea attributes including required, disabled, aria-describedby, and rows.
Invalid state maps to aria-invalid.
Keep resize behavior available unless a product workflow requires a fixed height.

items.name: label
items.title: Label
items.description: Provides an accessible name for a form control with a composable required marker and supplementary hint.
accessibility: Use htmlFor to associate the label with a control id.
Forwards refs and native label attributes.
Keep labels visible for reusable product forms.
Avoid relying on placeholder text as the only label.
Use LabelRow and LabelContent so the interactive hint is not nested inside the native label.
Pair LabelRequired with a native required control.
Tooltip guidance is supplementary and must not carry essential requirements.

items.name: form-message
items.title: FormMessage
items.description: Communicates help, validation, and error messages near a control.
accessibility: Associate messages with controls through aria-describedby.
Use danger tone for validation errors.
Set role="alert" only for active validation errors that should be announced.
Do not replace field-level errors with toasts.
items.usage:
import { FormMessage } from '@/components/nerio/components/form-message';

<FormMessage>Use at least 3 characters.</FormMessage>

items.name: field
items.title: Field
items.description: Composes a label, control, description, and validation message.
accessibility: Generates stable ids for label, description, and message relationships.
Clones a single React control child to wire id and aria-describedby, and only sets invalid state for invalid fields.
Preserves a child control id when supplied and merges existing aria-describedby values.
Uses role="alert" for message text only when invalid is true.
Supports one form control child; compose custom markup directly when a field needs multiple controls.
Keep helper text and error text close to the field.

items.name: form-group
items.title: FormGroup
items.description: Groups related fields or controls with an optional title, description, message, and layout.
accessibility: Renders a fieldset and uses legend when title is supplied.
Associates description and message text through aria-describedby.
Invalid state is explicit through invalid or aria-invalid and does not own validation logic.
Validation messages use role="alert" only when invalid is true.
Does not force a single control pattern or add form submission behavior.
items.usage:
import { FormGroup } from '@/components/nerio/components/form-group';

<FormGroup title="Notifications" description="Choose which updates should be sent by email.">Notification controls go here.</FormGroup>

items.name: checkbox
items.title: Checkbox
items.description: Toggles an independent form option.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Checkbox primitive for keyboard and checked-state behavior.
Provide a visible label or accessible name with aria-label or aria-labelledby.
Supports checked, defaultChecked, onCheckedChange, disabled, required, name, value, form, id, and aria-describedby through the Base UI root props.
Invalid state is explicit through invalid or aria-invalid and does not announce errors automatically.
Disabled state prevents interaction and reduces emphasis.
The check indicator renders through the Nerio icon adapter.
items.usage:
import { Checkbox } from '@/components/nerio/components/checkbox';

<label><Checkbox name="includeArchived" defaultChecked /> Include archived</label>

items.name: switch
items.title: Switch
items.description: Toggles an immediate binary setting.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Switch primitive for keyboard and checked-state behavior.
Use Switch for immediate on/off settings, not multi-select form choices.
Provide a visible label or accessible name with aria-label or aria-labelledby.
Supports checked, defaultChecked, onCheckedChange, disabled, required, name, value, form, id, and aria-describedby through the Base UI root props.
Disabled state prevents interaction and reduces emphasis.
Do not use Switch for long-running or destructive actions.
items.usage:
import { Switch } from '@/components/nerio/components/switch';

<label><Switch aria-label="Notify collaborators" defaultChecked /> Notify collaborators</label>

items.name: tabs
items.title: Tabs
items.description: Switches between related panels in the same context.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Tabs primitive.
Delegates keyboard navigation, selected state, and disabled tabs to Base UI.
Each tab is paired with a panel by value.
When defaultValue is omitted, the first enabled tab is selected; empty tab arrays render without runtime errors.
Supports controlled value, uncontrolled defaultValue, disabled tabs, and forwarded refs.
items.usage:
import { Tabs } from '@/components/nerio/components/tabs';

<Tabs tabs={[{ label: 'Overview', value: 'overview', content: '...' }, { label: 'Archive', value: 'archive', content: '...', disabled: true }]} />

items.name: breadcrumbs
items.title: Breadcrumbs
items.description: Shows hierarchy navigation with native anchors and a current page item.
accessibility: Renders a nav landmark with aria-label="Breadcrumb" by default.
Uses ordered list semantics for the hierarchy trail.
Marks the current page with aria-current="page".
Separators are decorative and hidden from assistive technology.
items.usage:
import { Breadcrumbs } from '@/components/nerio/components/breadcrumbs';

<Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: 'Docs', href: '/docs' }, { label: 'Components', href: '/docs/components' }, { label: 'Button' }]} />

items.name: pagination
items.title: Pagination
items.description: Provides basic previous, next, and page navigation without owning pagination state.
accessibility: Renders a nav landmark with aria-label="Pagination" by default.
Marks the current page with aria-current="page".
Disabled controls render as non-focusable text with aria-disabled; onSelect takes precedence over href when both are supplied.
Visible labels carry state; color is not the only indicator.
items.usage:
import { Pagination } from '@/components/nerio/components/pagination';

<Pagination previousHref="/docs/page/1" nextHref="/docs/page/3" pages={[{ key: '1', label: '1', href: '/docs/page/1' }, { key: '2', label: '2', href: '/docs/page/2', current: true }, { key: '3', label: '3', href: '/docs/page/3' }]} />

items.name: dialog
items.title: Dialog
items.description: Focuses a short decision or task above the current surface.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Dialog primitive.
Includes a title and optional description.
Focus is trapped while the dialog is open and returns to the trigger on close.
Includes an aria-label="Close dialog" close button rendered through the Nerio icon adapter.
Supports controlled open, uncontrolled defaultOpen, onOpenChange, and a forwarded popup ref.
items.usage:
import { Dialog } from '@/components/nerio/components/dialog';

<Dialog trigger="Share" title="Share collection" description="Choose collaborators and permissions.">...</Dialog>

items.name: popover
items.title: Popover
items.description: Reveals contextual controls or details near a trigger.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Popover primitive.
Keep essential form submission or destructive confirmation outside transient popovers.
Provide title or description when the content needs context.
Supports controlled open, uncontrolled defaultOpen, onOpenChange, and a forwarded popup ref.
items.usage:
import { Popover } from '@/components/nerio/components/popover';

<Popover trigger="Filters" title="View filters" description="Refine this list.">...</Popover>

items.name: dropdown-menu
items.title: DropdownMenu
items.description: Groups secondary commands behind a compact trigger.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Menu primitive.
Items are keyboard reachable and can be disabled.
Disabled items stay visible but cannot be selected.
Use destructive tone only for irreversible or high-risk commands.
Supports controlled open, uncontrolled defaultOpen, onOpenChange, and a forwarded menu popup ref.
items.usage:
import { DropdownMenu } from '@/components/nerio/components/dropdown-menu';

<DropdownMenu trigger="Actions" items={[{ label: 'Rename' }, { label: 'Duplicate', disabled: true }, { label: 'Archive', destructive: true }]} />

items.name: tooltip
items.title: Tooltip
items.description: Clarifies compact controls without carrying essential meaning.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Tooltip primitive.
Do not put required information only inside a tooltip.
Keep tooltip text short and specific.
Requires an element trigger. Use a keyboard-focusable control when keyboard users need to discover the tooltip.
Supports hover and keyboard focus behavior through Base UI, plus controlled open and disabled states when needed.
items.usage:
import { Tooltip } from '@/components/nerio/components/tooltip';

<Tooltip label="Copies the share link">...</Tooltip>

items.name: toast
items.title: Toast
items.description: Acknowledges short-lived product events without interrupting work.
accessibility: Uses the Base UI Toast primitive and manager.
Use low priority for routine confirmations and high priority only for urgent updates.
Static Toast content is separate from the managed status, copy, action, and dismiss layout; ToastViewport localizes visible dismiss text and its accessible label.
Keep messages concise and avoid replacing persistent error UI with a toast.
